subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Lightning Safety Science
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Lightning Safety Science.

hook.text: Lightning kills 20 Americans per year and injures hundreds. Most victims are struck outdoors.

buildup.text: Lightning doesn't just strike the tallest object—ground currents spread outward from the strike point.

discovery.text: Ground current causes the most lightning injuries—it travels through the earth and up through your feet.

twist.text: Being inside a car is safe—the metal body channels electricity around you, not through you.

climax.text: The 30-30 rule: if thunder follows lightning by less than 30 seconds, seek shelter immediately.

punchline.text: When thunder roars, go indoors. Ground current kills from below too.

quiz.question: What causes the most lightning injuries?
options: Ground current traveling through the earth and up through feet
